name: Spicey Chineese Eggplant
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Balsamic Vinegar or Black Chinkiang Vinegar
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and cut eggplants into 1 inch by 2 inch strips.
2. Mix sugar and balsamic vinegar in a bowl. Set aside.
3. Heat half of the olive oil in a wok over medium heat.
4. Add the diced eggplant to the hot oil and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
5. Remove the cooked eggplant from the pan using a slotted spoon and set aside.
6. Heat the remaining olive oil in the same wok over medium heat.
7. Add the remaining diced eggplant to the hot oil and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to remove this batch of eggplant as well.
8. Combine the cooked eggplant with the first batch in the pan.
9. Stir in half a teaspoon of red pepper flakes, or more to taste, and continue cooking for one minute longer.
10. Remove the pan from heat.
11. Add the balsamic vinegar mixture (sugar and vinegar) to the pan and stir until fully absorbed by the eggplant.
12. Sprinkle chopped parsley over the dish and serve immediately over hot rice.
